frameworks.name: intuitive theories of physics, psychology, and causality
frameworks.one_line: Moral judgment grounded in mental simulation over intuitive physics and causal reasoning.

frameworks.name: psychophysical / qualitative moral judgment models
frameworks.one_line: Prior models that fit data but miss underlying inferential concepts.

core_question: Can a quantitative computational model based on intuitive physics, psychology, and causality predict moral judgments of harm and help?
key_assumptions: people infer agent desire and intent from physical scene dynamics
moral judgment can be reduced to inferences over intuitive theories
causal structure mediates attribution of harm or help
manipulations: scenarios where an agent brings about or prevents harm
scenarios involving helping versus harming another agent
key_findings: model qualitatively reproduces empirical findings of prior moral-judgment work
model quantitatively predicts judgments in new harm/help scenarios
interpretations: moral judgment is a generative inference over intuitive physics, psychology, and causality
underlying inferential concepts, not stimulus features, drive moral evaluations
